secondary_archetype: VP Digital Strategy & Growth (executive commercial/digital leadership)

level_signals: Comp range $235K-$305K consistent with senior executive
Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing digital leadership team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 15+ years of leadership experience across digital, commerce, marketing, customer experience, or related functions within beauty, retail, consumer products, or high-growth consumer brands
Proven track record of developing and executing enterprise-level digital growth strategies that drive meaningful business impact
Experience leading large-scale digital transformation, omnichannel customer experience, or digital capability-building initiatives
Strong commercial and financial acumen with the ability to evaluate growth opportunities, investment decisions, and long-term business outcomes
Demonstrated success leading senior teams and developing high-performing leaders across multiple disciplines
Deep understanding of evolving digital ecosystems, including commerce, marketplaces, social platforms, customer engagement technologies, data strategy, personalization, and emerging technologies
Strong executive presence with the ability to influence and align stakeholders across all levels of an organization
Exceptional strategic thinking and problem-solving skills, with the ability to connect customer insights, market trends, and business objectives into actionable plans
Proven ability to operate effectively in highly cross-functional environments and drive alignment across diverse stakeholder groups
Bachelor's degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree required; MBA or advanced degree preferred

scope.surface: Enterprise-wide digital growth strategy across owned, earned, and paid digital channels; Digital Commerce, Social Commerce, Omnichannel Customer Experience, Loyalty, Brand Marketing, Technology alignment
primary_metrics_inferred: Brand relevance and engagement across digital touchpoints
core_responsibilities: Define and lead Glossier's long-term digital strategy, ensuring alignment with company growth objectives, customer priorities, and brand ambitions
Develop a cohesive vision for how customers discover, engage with, and purchase from Glossier across all digital touchpoints and channels
Identify and evaluate emerging digital commerce opportunities, technologies, platforms, and consumer behaviors that can unlock future growth and competitive advantage
Establish enterprise-wide digital growth priorities and investment frameworks, partnering with executive leadership to determine resource allocation and strategic initiatives
Lead the evolution of Glossier's omnichannel customer experience strategy

Partner with Technology leadership to define the digital capability roadmap, including platform modernization, customer data strategy, personalization capabilities, and future-state digital infrastructure
Champion customer-centric decision making across the organization
Serve as the executive sponsor for digital transformation initiatives
Partner with Brand Marketing and Creative leadership to ensure digital innovation supports and enhances Glossier's brand positioning
Develop executive-level reporting and performance frameworks
Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing digital leadership team
Stay ahead of industry trends across digital commerce, AI, customer engagement, marketplaces, social commerce, retail technology, and emerging consumer behaviors

green_flags: Equity in the form of RSUs mentioned
Reporting directly to Chief Commercial Officer — high organizational visibility
Clear articulation of scope and cross-functional partners
Mission-driven brand with strong cultural identity
red_flags: Location not specified anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite and in which city
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not addressed
Buzzword-heavy JD with limited specificity on measurable outcomes or technology platforms used
15+ years required creates a narrow candidate pool at a very senior level
No mention of team size or direct reports structure

ambiguities: The JD does not specify a work location (city, remote eligibility, or office requirement). Location fields left as 'unclear'.
The Bachelor's degree requirement is listed in the same bullet as 'MBA or advanced degree preferred' — Bachelor's treated as hard requirement, MBA treated as nice-to-have soft.
